events.title: Parma Calcio 1913 vs. AS Roma - Halftime Result
events.description: In the upcoming Serie A game between Parma Calcio 1913 and AS Roma, scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET: This event contains halftime result markets for home, draw, and away outcomes within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin halftime result market for Parma Calcio 1913 versus AS Roma in Serie A, with all outcomes—Parma lead, draw, Roma lead—at 50% implied probability, underscoring the matchup's even dynamics at Stadio Ennio Tardini. Parma's home advantage bolsters their case after a 1-0 clean-sheet win over Pisa on April 25, extending an unbeaten run that includes a 1-1 draw at Lazio, showcasing defensive resilience amid mid-table positioning around 12th. Roma, sitting 6th, mirrors the momentum with a 2-0 away victory at Bologna that same day following a 1-1 home draw against Atalanta, but their recent head-to-head halftime stalemate (0-0 in October 2025's 2-1 win) highlights frequent low-scoring first halves. Absent major injury disruptions or lineup shocks, cautious starts and Serie A trends keep probabilities tightly bunched.

markets.question: Metamask FDV above $500M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Metamask's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Metamask doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Another US debt downgrade before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States' long-term sovereign credit letter rating is downgraded by any of the three major credit rating agencies (S&P, Moody's, Fitch) at any point by December 31, 2026 11:59pm ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Standard & Poor's, Moody's, or Fitch, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Another US debt downgrade before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States' long-term sovereign credit letter rating is downgraded by any of the three major credit rating agencies (S&P, Moody's, Fitch) at any point by December 31, 2026 11:59pm ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Standard & Poor's, Moody's, or Fitch, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 77% implied probability against another US sovereign credit rating downgrade before 2027, reflecting stable outlooks from Moody's (Aa1), S&P (AA+), and Fitch (AA+) as affirmed through late April 2026. Robust US GDP growth has offset fiscal deterioration, including debt held by the public surpassing GDP for the first time since World War II in April 2026 and projected fiscal year 2026 deficits near 6% of GDP per CBO estimates. Despite rising Treasury borrowing needs exceeding $2 trillion this year, agencies cite the dollar's reserve currency status and deep capital markets as supportive factors, with no recent negative revisions or debt ceiling brinkmanship shifting sentiment. Key catalysts ahead include mid-year rating reviews and Congressional Budget Office updates on debt trajectory to 120% of GDP by 2036.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump's tie be another color on May 9, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on the predominant color of the tie worn by Donald Trump during his first public appearance during which he wears a tie on the specified date (ET).

The color will be determined based solely on the first publicly available photo or video of the appearance. Reporting of the tie color without photos or videos will not be considered. Subsequent publication of other photos or videos, including those taken earlier, will not affect the resolution.

For the tie to be considered predominantly blue or red, its primary color must be a shade of blue or red, though it may feature other colors as part of a pattern. 

This tie would count as "Blue": 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/donald-trump-tie-color-blue-5ywRT5OXfn-5.jpg

This tie would count as "Red": 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/donald-trump-tie-color-red-a7ObxtpGQcrR.jpg

The following tie has no distinguishable predominant color, and thus would resolve to "Other": 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/donald-trump-tie-color-other-Q8aIgOP7wFs1.jpg

If Donald Trump does not have any public appearance on the specified date, does not wear a tie, or if it is a color not listed among the strike options, the market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will US unemployment reach at least 5.0% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any seasonally adjusted unemployment rate (total unemployed, as a percent of the civilian labor force, official unemployment rate denoted as U-3) reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics in an “Employment Situation Report” for a reference month in 2026 is greater than or equal to the listed percentage. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The relevant reports for this market are the Employment Situation Reports for January-December, 2026. This market may not resolve to “No” until the Employment Situation report for December 2026 is released. If no Employment Situation Report for December 2026 is released by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, however, this market will resolve based on all previously published data up to that time. 

The resolution source for this market is the Monthly Employment Situation Report, published by the BLS every month at https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/empsit.htm, specifically the U-3 measure in Table A-15 for each month.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports unemployment to one decimal point. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The relevant reports for this market are the Employment Situation Reports for January-December, 2026. eventMetadata.context_description: The US unemployment rate held steady at 4.3 percent in April 2026 per the latest Bureau of Labor Statistics report, with nonfarm payrolls rising a modest 115,000 amid cooling job growth after little net change over the prior year. This resilience reflects a labor market adjusting to higher interest rates, with part-time employment for economic reasons climbing but overall stability anchoring trader consensus around low-4 percent levels for the year. The Federal Reserve's March 2026 dot plot median projects 4.4 percent by Q4, aligning with IMF and OECD forecasts near 4.4 percent, though some analysts eye risks up to 4.8 percent if GDP growth falters. Polymarket traders price in this skin-in-the-game view, with the May jobs report due early June as the next key catalyst alongside FOMC updates.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the CA-13 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the CA-13 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: SSC Napoli vs. Bologna FC 1909: O/U 1.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Serie A game between SSC Napoli and Bologna FC 1909, scheduled for May 11 at 2:45 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if SSC Napoli and Bologna FC 1909 combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on legaseriea.it. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: SSC Napoli vs. Bologna FC 1909 - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Serie A game, scheduled for May 11 at 2:45 PM ET.

markets.question: Will Russia capture all of Donetsk Oblast by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia militarily captures the entirety of Donetsk Oblast by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET.

Donetsk Oblast will be considered captured when the entirety of the below-specified municipalities is simultaneously shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If those areas are not simultaneously shaded red by the resolution date, this market will resolve to “No”.

The relevant municipalities in Donetsk Oblast that must be simultaneously captured in their entirety are: Kramatorsk, Sloviansk, Kostiantynivka, Druzhkivka, Pokrovsk, Dobropillia, Oleksandrivka, Cherkaske, and Sviatohirsk.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of a municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities with a darker grey line. If all area within a municipality is shaded in red, however, the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this may still qualify.

Once Russia has captured the entirety of the above-specified municipalities simultaneously, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap is rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will not qualify, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. Similarly, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Control must be established through military conquest.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces continue incremental advances in Donetsk Oblast, with recent geolocated progress near Horykhove, Rodynske, and Pishchane as of early May 2026, per DeepState maps, amid intensified infiltration tactics and assaults in the Pokrovsk and Kostiantynivka directions. Ukrainian defenses repelled persistent Russian attempts to breach Kostiantynivka's southern outskirts, where fighting reached within one kilometer, while conducting counterattacks and strikes on Russian logistics depots and command posts in occupied areas. Russia suffered a net territorial loss across the theater in April—the first since August 2024—highlighting slowed momentum despite capturing 1,700 square kilometers overall in 2026. Ukraine retains control of roughly 15-17% of the oblast, including the fortified "fortress belt" of Sloviansk, Kramatorsk, and Kostiantynivka, posing major barriers to full Russian seizure absent major breakthroughs or diplomatic shifts.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects persistent uncertainty in Venezuela's leadership transition four months after U.S. forces captured Nicolás Maduro on January 3, 2026, positioning him at 59.4% to remain head of state by year-end despite his detention in U.S. custody. Delcy Rodríguez, appointed interim president by the Supreme Court and backed by military and police pledges of loyalty, holds 27.5% as chavismo continuity endures amid constitutional disputes over succession and no elections scheduled. Opposition leader María Corina Machado trails at 7.5%, hampered by her exile and recent failed return attempts, including an April Madrid rally. Recent U.S. easing of sanctions and debt restructuring approval on May 5 underscore interim stability but highlight stalled democratic shifts.

markets.question: Will Mike Kennealy win the 2026 Massachusetts Governor Republican primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Massachusetts, scheduled to take place on September 1, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Massachusetts Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Massachusetts Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Minogue's landslide victory at the Massachusetts Republican Party convention on April 26, capturing 70% of delegate votes for the party's official endorsement, has solidified his position as the clear frontrunner in trader consensus at 81% implied probability for the September 1 GOP gubernatorial primary. Former Housing Secretary Mike Kennealy suspended his campaign after failing the 15% threshold to qualify, dropping his odds to 1%, while former MBTA head Brian Shortsleeve barely advanced with 15.5% but trails amid Minogue's dominant fundraising—nearly $10 million cash on hand—and a reported 13-point polling lead. Shortsleeve's recent calls for debates highlight policy contrasts on affordability and abortion, yet party momentum favors the biotech executive outsider ahead of the primary ballot battle.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in San Francisco be between 52-53°F on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the San Francisco International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the San Francisco International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ca/san-francisco/KSFO.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service forecasts and recent observations from San Francisco International Airport (KSFO), the key recording station, project a high temperature near 65°F on May 9 amid mostly sunny skies and west winds of 10-15 mph, driving trader consensus toward 64-65°F at 48.5% implied probability. Persistent marine layer from cool Pacific sea surface temperatures around 55°F tempers warming, but expected burn-off by midday supports this range over cooler outcomes, while limiting escalation to 68°F or higher (8.5%). Yesterday's high near 60°F reflects denser stratus; today's ridge of high pressure enhances solar heating slightly above recent norms (May average ~65°F). Updated hourly data through afternoon will clarify peak, with model consensus showing low uncertainty for mid-60s.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick (R) holds a commanding fundraising lead with over $7.6 million cash on hand entering the May 19 Democratic primary, where Bucks County Commissioner Bob Harvie leads Lucia Simonelli as the likely challenger in this D+1 battleground district pivotal to Democrats' House majority flip path amid Republicans' slim 218-214 edge. Trader consensus tilts slightly Democratic at 52.5% despite a March poll showing Fitzpatrick ahead 48-42 over Harvie, reflecting Fitzpatrick's recent bipartisan votes—including support for Haitian TPS extension on April 16 and Canada tariff repeal—that have drawn GOP primary ire and depressed base enthusiasm. Separation could arise from the primary outcome, national midterm headwinds, or heavy outside spending targeting Bucks County's swing voters.

markets.question: Will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit (HIGH) $7.00 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) shares traded around $5 following a Q1 2026 earnings miss, with revenue of $720 million falling short of $984 million consensus estimates and EPS at -$0.18 versus -$0.10 expected, prompting a 6% single-day decline amid persistent housing market headwinds. Despite the top-line shortfall and $173 million net loss, operational metrics strengthened—homes purchased surged 45% quarter-over-quarter to 2,474 units, gross margins expanded to 10%, and contribution margins reached 4.4%, with adjusted EBITDA profitability achieved on a go-forward basis by April 1. Inventory aged favorably while Q2 revenue is guided 25% higher. Analyst consensus holds a "Hold" rating with a $4.50 average price target, though a fresh $8 Buy initiation highlights potential iBuying recovery; traders eye May housing data and mortgage rate trends for near-term catalysts.

markets.question: Israel x Hezbollah diplomatic meeting by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of Israel and Hezbollah by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of Israel and Hezbollah who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding Israel-Hezbollah relations on behalf of their governments/leadership structures. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the government of Israel and Hezbollah leadership, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hezbollah leaders, including Secretary General Naim Qassem, have explicitly rejected direct diplomatic negotiations with Israel as "futile," while signaling openness to indirect talks amid a fragile US-Iran truce. US-mediated ambassadorial meetings between Israel and Lebanon—two prior rounds in Washington, one attended by President Trump—center on Hezbollah disarmament, border security, and comprehensive peace to enforce Lebanese state authority, explicitly excluding the group. Israel's May 7 airstrike killing a Radwan Forces commander in Beirut suburbs underscores ongoing escalation despite diplomacy. A third round of talks, including technical teams, is set for May 14-15, but direct Israel-Hezbollah engagement faces steep barriers from public rejections and military posturing.

markets.description:

The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the SOL/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/sol-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream SOL/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carlos Alcaraz's withdrawal from the 2026 French Open due to a lingering right wrist injury—announced April 24—has propelled world No. 1 Jannik Sinner to a dominant 71.5% implied probability as trader consensus favorite for the Roland Garros title, completing his career Grand Slam. Sinner reinforced this positioning by capturing the Madrid Masters last weekend, defeating Alexander Zverev 6-1, 6-2 in the final for his record fifth straight Masters 1000 crown and showcasing enhanced clay-court variety with drop shots and net play. Zverev (6.8%) follows as the top clay specialist with a Rome title defense looming, while Novak Djokovic (4.3%) lags amid sparse recent clay match wins at age 39. Rising Spaniard Rafael Jodar (4.2%), a 19-year-old ATP No. 34 who pushed Sinner in Madrid semis, and French hopefuls Arthur Fils (3.5%) and Lorenzo Musetti (2.3%) draw next-tier support amid a depleted field featuring injuries to Jack Draper and Taylor Fritz. Sinner's mental fortitude in best-of-five sets remains key, though upsets or his own fitness issues could shift dynamics.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 76.5% on U.S. enactment of an AI safety bill before 2027, reflecting stalled federal legislative progress amid partisan gridlock in the 119th Congress. Despite over 150 AI-related bills introduced, including the AI Guardrails Act and Future of Artificial Intelligence Innovation Act, none have advanced beyond committee referral as of early May 2026. The Trump administration's March 20 National AI Legislative Framework prioritizes innovation, voluntary standards, child safety measures, and state law preemption over stringent safety regulations, aligning with Republican House and Senate leadership. States like Washington and Oregon have enacted narrower AI laws, filling the federal void, while upcoming 2026 midterms and lame-duck session add uncertainty to passage prospects.

The Baltimore Ravens follow at 47% following Njoku's March meeting there, where they eyed pairing him with Mark Andrews after losing Isaiah Likely, bolstering their AFC North contender TE room. A cluster of teams including Arizona Cardinals, Denver Broncos, Miami Dolphins, and Atlanta Falcons hover around 46%, reflecting broad demand for Njoku's Pro Bowl production amid a thin free agency tight end market post-NFL Draft. Cleveland Browns lag at 24.5% after his February farewell announcement signaling no return.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: In New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election on April 16, trader consensus overwhelmingly prices Analilia Mejia's margin of victory at 20-25% following near-final tallies showing her defeating Republican Joe Hathaway 60.2% to 39.4% (81,825 to 53,520 votes), a 20.8-point edge aligning precisely with that bucket. The Democratic-leaning district (D+5 baseline), robust early voting (over 39,000 ballots), and Mejia's grassroots momentum from her upset February primary win drove the lopsided result in a low-turnout contest. With no recounts requested and vote counts stable since election night, resolution awaits official certification; shifts below 20% would require improbable surges in outstanding provisional or mail-in ballots.

markets.question: Bank of England decreases interest rates by 50+ bps after July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in basis points in the Bank Rate resulting from the July 2026 meeting of the Bank of England’s Monetary Policy Committee, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source will be official information from the Bank of England, including the statement or release from its July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting, scheduled for July 30, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of England calendar (https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/monetary-policy/upcoming-mpc-dates). This market may resolve as soon as the statement or release of the Bank of England's July 2026 Monetary Policy Committee meeting with relevant data is issued.

If the specified rate is defined by an upper and lower bound, the relevant change will be the change to the upper bound.

If the specified rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded according to the following guidelines. Increases or decreases of less than 25 bps will be rounded to 25 bps (e.g. an increase or decrease of 10 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 25 bps). Increases or decreases of greater than 25 bps will be rounded to the nearest 25 bps and will be rounded away from 0 in cases of equidistance (e.g., an increase or decrease of 37.5 bps would be considered to be an increase or decrease of 50 bps). Displayed options of “Increase” or “Decrease” will include policy rate increases or decreases of any size.

If the specified meeting is postponed to a date and time before the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve based on the outcome of that postponed meeting. If the specified meeting is cancelled, or postponed such that no decision is announced by the start of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the “No Change” bracket. Emergency changes to the specified rate not resulting from the specified meeting will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price evenly matched implied probabilities of 49.5% across all Bank of England July Monetary Policy Committee outcomes—from 25 basis points or 50+ bps hikes/cuts to no change—reflecting deep uncertainty over the rate path amid conflicting signals. March 2026 CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year from 3.0%, fueled by energy and motor fuel spikes from Middle East conflict, leading to a unanimous MPC hold at 3.75% Bank Rate. Resilient labor conditions, with unemployment falling to 4.9% in February, counterbalance disinflation forces, while persistent services inflation sustains hike risks. Pivotal catalysts include today's April 30 decision, upcoming May/June CPI data, and energy price developments ahead of the July 30 meeting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Columbus Blue Jackets defenseman Zach Werenski at 68% implied probability for the James Norris Memorial Trophy following the NHL's May 7 announcement naming him, Buffalo Sabres captain Rasmus Dahlin, and Colorado Avalanche star Cale Makar as the 2025-26 finalists. Werenski's league-second 81 points (22 goals, 59 assists) in 75 games, topped by his NHL-leading 47 even-strength points and 17 even-strength goals, plus a +32 rating and 24:51 average ice time, underscore his all-around dominance on a non-playoff Blue Jackets squad. Dahlin's 74 points, top rankings in hits and 5-on-5 puck battles, and Sabres playoff push sustain his 27% standing, while two-time winner Makar's 79 points lag amid Avalanche postseason priorities.

eventMetadata.context_description: Abdul El-Sayed leads trader consensus at 53.5% implied probability to win Michigan's August 4 Democratic U.S. Senate primary for the open seat vacated by retiring Sen. Gary Peters, buoyed by his dominance among young voters under 40 and Black Democrats in recent Emerson and Glengariff polls from late April, where he tied or edged Mallory McMorrow around 24% while Rep. Haley Stevens trailed at 13-25%. El-Sayed's progressive platform and alliances like Bernie Sanders have fueled his prediction market surge since mid-April, despite McMorrow's strength with older voters and women. Sen. Debbie Stabenow's May 6 endorsement of Stevens aims to rally establishment support, but high undecideds (35-36%) keep the race fluid ahead of summer campaigning.

markets.question: No change in Bank of Japan’s interest rates after the July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The Statement on Monetary Policy for the Bank of Japan's Monetary Policy meeting for July is scheduled to be released on July 31, 2026 (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm).

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the short-term policy interest rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of Japan's July 2026 meeting.

If the short-term policy interest rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of Japan (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Japan's statement for the specified meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump post 120-139 Truth Social posts from May 8 to May 15, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Donald Trump (@realDonaldTrump), posts on Truth Social between May 8, 12:00 PM ET and May 15, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, Truth Social itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects Donald Trump's consistent Truth Social posting average of 18-22 per day in 2025-early 2026, including 6,168 posts last year and 565 in April, yielding expectations of 144-176 over the May 8-15 window—yet probabilities cluster tightly at 23% for 120-139 and 21.5% for 100-119 due to high variability from late-night sprees and news-driven barrages, as seen in the prior week's 146 posts (May 1-8). Recent Iran policy posts and ongoing conflict signals sustain elevated volume, but presidential travel or quiet diplomatic periods could trim output, while escalations like airstrikes or summits might trigger 160+ tallies, preventing clear separation among top bins.

markets.question: Will New Slovenia – Christian Democrats (NSi) be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

events.title: Which parties will be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
events.description:

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful vote of confidence in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Slovenia's National Assembly elections on March 22 produced a hung parliament requiring 46 seats for a majority, with Freedom Movement (GS) securing 29 seats, Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) 28, NSi–SLS–FOKUS coalition 9, Democrats (DEM) 6, and others filling the rest. Incumbent PM Robert Golob's coalition talks collapsed by April 20, leading him to concede and enter opposition. President Nataša Pirc Musar declined a PM nominee after first-round consultations on April 25, initiating a second round focused on SDS leader Janez Janša's center-right negotiations. Recent progress includes Janša's May 5 announcement of agreed coalition guidelines and parliament's passage of a Government Act trimming ministries to 14 with 49 votes, signaling potential majority support. Upcoming PM nomination and confidence vote will determine the next government's parties.

markets.question: Will Natural Gas (NG) hit (LOW) $2.00 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of Natural Gas futures has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For Natural Gas futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For Natural Gas (NG) futures, the last trading day is defined as the third last business day of the month prior to the contract month, consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month Natural Gas futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=NGD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month Natural Gas (NG) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Natural Gas (NG) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Natural Gas (NG) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Henry Hub natural gas spot prices hover around $2.70 per million British thermal units (MMBtu) following the latest EIA weekly storage report, which showed a smaller-than-expected injection of 63 billion cubic feet (Bcf) for the week ending May 1—below consensus forecasts of 72 Bcf—bolstering trader sentiment amid elevated inventories at 2,205 Bcf. June NYMEX futures trade near $2.75/MMBtu, reflecting balanced supply growth of 2% year-over-year in 2026 production and surging LNG exports, offset by emerging summer power sector cooling demand. Recent output declines and milder weather have propped up near-term pricing, with key catalysts including the May 14 EIA storage release and extended weather forecasts that could amplify air conditioning load.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump publicly insult Friedrich Merz by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump makes any public statement in which he insults, mocks, or attacks the listed individual personally or professionally in a clearly negative manner between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

This includes calling the individual weak, stupid, disloyal, a failure, using an insulting nickname, using other derogatory language, or using the negative form of a positive trait in a derogatory personal way (e.g., “He/She isn’t smart”). Negative forms used in reference to the individual's professional actions, policies, or decisions (e.g., “He/She isn’t being smart about this policy”) will not count. Policy disagreements stated without disparaging language will not count.

A direct reference will qualify even if the individual is not named, so long as it is reasonably clear from context that they are the subject.

Any written, verbal, or recorded public statement by Trump qualifies.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene at 80% implied probability as Trump's next public insult target by May 31, driven by her recent vocal opposition to administration spending priorities and internal GOP fractures over fiscal policy amid debt ceiling pressures. Tucker Carlson trails at 68%, reflecting Trump's history of assailing conservative media critics following Tucker's segments questioning White House Iran escalation narratives. German CDU leader Friedrich Merz sits at 66%, tied to transatlantic tensions on NATO burden-sharing and trade tariffs. Recent Truth Social activity—including a May 5 Pope Leo XIV attack over Iran remarks, a dumpster meme targeting Fed Chair Powell, and Cinco de Mayo jabs at Democrats—highlights Trump's pattern, with White House Rose Garden remarks and potential rallies as upcoming catalysts through month's end.

markets.question: Will Chicago Sky win the 2026 WNBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is determined as the champion of the WNBA for the 2026 season. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed participant to named as the champion of WNBA for the 2026 season per the rules of WNBA (e.g., participant is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after November 14, 11:59 PM ET, or a champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the WNBA: however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Liberty lead trader consensus at 30.5% implied probability for the 2026 WNBA title following their acquisition of three-time All-Star Satou Sabally in free agency, bolstering a core featuring Breanna Stewart and Sabrina Ionescu that reached the 2025 Finals. Atlanta Dream sit close at 25.5% after trading for Angel Reese at a bargain—ceding only future first-round picks—while retaining franchise-record 30-win contributors Allisha Gray and Rhyne Howard. Indiana Fever (18%) and defending champions Las Vegas Aces (16%) round out the tight top four, with Caitlin Clark-Kelsey Mitchell-Aliyah Boston health and A'ja Wilson's dynasty bid drawing bets amid league parity from the April draft, roster cuts finalized yesterday, and expansion to 15 teams diluting depth but elevating multiple contenders.

markets.question: Will Putin visit China by May 31?
markets.description:
If Vladimir Putin visits China by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Putin physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of China. Whether or not Putin enters Chinese airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on this market's resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from Vladimir Putin, the Federal Government of Russia, and the Chinese government; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kremlin aide Yury Ushakov stated on May 7 that dates for President Vladimir Putin's visit to China have been agreed upon, with a joint announcement planned alongside Beijing to avoid any "unfriendly" unilateral disclosure, signaling advanced preparations just weeks from the May 31 deadline. This follows Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's April 15 confirmation of a trip in the first half of 2026 during talks with President Xi Jinping, who urged deeper strategic coordination, and a Kremlin report on April 29 affirming the schedule amid strengthening bilateral ties on energy and multipolar world order. Recent TASS updates describe the summit as "just around the corner," reflecting trader consensus on high likelihood despite no exact date yet, with potential for diplomatic delays in a tense global context.

markets.question: Spread: AD Municipal Liberia (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Liga Promerica game, scheduled for May 10 at 6:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "AD Municipal Liberia" if AD Municipal Liberia win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Deportivo Saprissa".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on unafut.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Deportivo Saprissa vs. AD Municipal Liberia - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Liga Promerica game, scheduled for May 10 at 6:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Deportivo Saprissa, second in the Primera División Clausura standings with 31 points from 17 matches, host AD Municipal Liberia, who sit third on 30 points after a stunning 2-1 upset victory at Estadio Ricardo Saprissa Aymá on April 12—Liberia's first win there in 23 head-to-head meetings, ending Saprissa's unbeaten streak despite their prior 5-1 cup rout on March 29. Saprissa's recent form shows prolific scoring (10 goals in last five), but defensive lapses allowed late concessions, boosting Liberia's confidence amid the tight title race. No major injuries reported from official team updates; home advantage and historical dominance (19 wins, 7 draws, 2 losses overall) favor Saprissa rebound, though Liberia's away resilience adds upset potential for props like total goals (averaging 3.21 H2H).

markets.question: Will the US acquire part of Greenland in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States acquires control of any land territory that is part of Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only the transfer of sovereignty, or the acquisition of primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control qualifies.

1. Transfer of Sovereignty: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument results in a defined area of Greenland coming under the formal sovereignty of the U.S. (e.g., incorporated as a U.S. state, territory, possession, or other U.S. political classification), even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline.

2. Acquisition of Primary or Exclusive Jurisdiction or Control: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument establishes a defined area in Greenland in which the U.S. has primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over the territory, such that the ordinary legal authority of Denmark and Greenland do not apply,except by U.S. permission. Such agreements or instruments will qualify even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline. 

3. Use of Force: If the U.S. acquires primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over a defined area of Greenland through force, this will also qualify. 

An announcement will qualify only if it is accompanied by or consists of a binding agreement or legal instrument  (e.g., enacted legislation, a signed treaty, the signed text of an agreement, or an executive action implementing such an agreement) that unambiguously creates a transfer of sovereignty, or primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control,  even if this transfer or acquisition takes effect after the market deadline. 

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. Basing rights, access agreements, SOFA-type arrangements, COFA-type arrangements, commercial concessions, or other permissions to use land (including leases) will not alone qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction or control  in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying control.

Examples of qualifying events include but are not limited to treaty or piece of legislation that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession, even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States, Denmark, and Greenland; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's January 2026 revival of interest in acquiring Greenland for national security amid Arctic competition with Russia and China sparked initial speculation, but trader consensus at 86.5% "No" reflects firm Danish and Greenlandic rejections of any sovereignty transfer. Greenland leaders, prioritizing full independence from Denmark—bolstered by pro-independence Naleraq party's Danish parliament seat in March—have repeatedly stated the territory is not for sale. No formal negotiations or concessions have advanced since early-year rhetoric and a symbolic US congressional bill, constrained by the 1951 Defense Agreement recognizing Danish control and NATO alliance dynamics. A planned US envoy visit to Nuuk on May 19 offers little prospect of breakthrough absent mutual consent.

markets.description: In the upcoming Ligue 1 game between Paris Saint-Germain FC and Stade Brestois 29, scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Bradley Barcola is credited with a goal in the official box score within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Paris Saint-Germain FC vs. Stade Brestois 29 - Player Props
events.description: Goal scorers for the Ligue 1 game between Paris Saint-Germain FC and Stade Brestois 29, scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paris Saint-Germain holds a six-point Ligue 1 lead over Lens entering week 33, hosting Stade Brestois at Parc des Princes on May 10 amid title-clinching pressure with just three matches left, including a May 13 showdown versus Lens. Brest impressed in a 3-3 thriller draw at home against Lens on April 25—racing to a 3-0 lead before conceding late—and followed with a resilient 0-0 at Paris FC on May 3, bolstering their mid-table standing. PSG's attack thrives on home soil where they've dominated Brest historically (3-0 and 7-0 wins last season), but injuries sideline key fullbacks Achraf Hakimi (hamstring) and Nuno Mendes (hamstring) through mid-May, thinning defensive depth and potentially boosting Brest counter-threats. Traders monitor PSG forwards' recent scoring streaks against Brest's exposed backline for goal and assist props, alongside rotation risks late-season.

markets.question: Will Kim Boo-kyum win the 2026 Daegu mayoral election?
markets.description:
The Daegu mayoral election is scheduled to take place on June 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Interim, temporary, or caretaker mayors will not count.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission (https://www.nec.go.kr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors People Power Party lawmaker Choo Kyung-ho at 63% implied probability to win the Daegu mayoral race on June 3, reflecting Daegu's status as a conservative stronghold despite recent polls showing a dead heat with Democratic Party former Prime Minister Kim Boo-kyum. Polls from early May, including Daegu MBC (May 2-3: Kim 46%, Choo 42%) and JTBC-Metavoice (May 5-6: Choo 41%, Kim 40%), indicate races within margins of error, with Choo gaining from regional backlash to the Democratic Party's special prosecutor bill. Both candidates recently pledged economic revitalization—Choo via Samsung partnerships, Kim through industrial transformation—while minor candidates trail far behind, underscoring the two-way battleground dynamic.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the WI-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WI-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Glenn Grothman holds a commanding position in Wisconsin's 6th Congressional District, a reliably red seat with an R+8 partisan voting index, driving trader consensus to an 78% implied probability for the Republican Party amid the 2026 midterms. Grothman's dominant 2024 general election win at 61% and unopposed primary path underscore incumbency advantages in safe districts, where historical base rates favor the party of the president in non-competitive races. A crowded Democratic primary field—including Bradley Smith, Aaron Wojciechowski, and others with modest fundraising—lacks a clear frontrunner, fragmenting opposition as nomination papers circulate ahead of the June 1 filing deadline and August 11 primaries. Independent challengers like Mike Thurow add minimal threat, with no recent polls or scandals altering the landscape in the past 30 days.

events.title: Sunderland AFC vs. Manchester United FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Premier League game, scheduled for May 9 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester United hold third place in the Premier League table, pushing for Champions League qualification, but arrive at Stadium of Light hampered by a defensive injury crisis: Matthijs de Ligt sidelined long-term with back issues, Lisandro Martínez doubtful, Leny Yoro nursing a knock, Patrick Dorgu out with hamstring problems, and Harry Maguire's recent suspension history adding uncertainty to the backline. Sunderland, mid-table after 12 wins from 32 matches, stunned Tottenham 1-0 at home on April 12 but suffered a humiliating 0-5 home loss to Nottingham Forest last weekend and a 3-4 defeat at Aston Villa prior, testing home form against United's superior quality despite their earlier 2-0 league win this season. Captain Granit Xhaka's ankle concern and Romaine Mundle's season-ending hamstring absence loom for the hosts.

City's home dominance and 4-1-0 record in the last five head-to-heads underscore their edge, though defensive absences like Rodri (groin) and Ruben Dias (hamstring) introduce uncertainty for prop markets on clean sheets or goals.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 17°C or below on May 9?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 9 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Trump speak to Elon Musk in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual speaks with Donald Trump between May 1 and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Speaking is defined as any verbal interaction between the listed individual and Donald Trump, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

The resolution source will be credible media reporting. However, in the absence of definitive or in the case of contradictory media reporting, statements by either of the specified individuals or their official representatives will also be considered.

Statements by the specified individuals or their official representatives will only be considered conclusive if the claims explicitly, or in context unambiguously, indicate that a qualifying talk occurred within the specified timeframe and took place via verbal communication, and provided that such claims are not contradicted by the other specified individual, their representatives, or credible media reporting by the end of the third calendar date (ET) following the statement in question.

If the date/time of a qualifying talk cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day following this market's above-specified timeframe, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether it was later confirmed to have taken place.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Trump administration's May 2 announcement of a unilateral withdrawal of 5,000 US troops from Germany over the next six to 12 months—without prior NATO coordination—has heightened alliance tensions and fueled speculation on US commitment, prompting NATO officials to seek operational details amid concerns over collective defense. However, full US withdrawal from NATO faces steep legal barriers under Section 1250A of the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act, requiring two-thirds Senate approval or congressional legislation, which remains absent. No official White House statements confirm exit plans, and recent troop repositioning reflects broader foreign policy shifts toward the Pacific rather than dissolution. Traders weigh these structural hurdles against diplomatic frictions, with upcoming NATO summits and European burden-sharing debates as key catalysts.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

This market will resolve to 'Aryna Sabalenka' if Aryna Sabalenka advances against Sorana Cirstea.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka holds a commanding edge entering her third-round clash with No. 26 Sorana Cirstea at the Internazionali BNL d'Italia, following a clinical 6-2, 6-3 second-round dismissal of former French Open champion Barbora Krejcikova on clay. Cirstea, the 36-year-old Romanian veteran, advanced comfortably 6-2, 6-0 over Tatjana Maria, marking a solid start to her Rome campaign where she has reached the fourth round twice before. Sabalenka leads their head-to-head 2-1, including straight-sets wins in Brisbane 2026 (hard) and Madrid 2023 (clay), leveraging her baseline power despite clay's slower conditions. No reported injuries for either, though Cirstea's experience in extended rallies could test Sabalenka's error rate on the Foro Italico surface, with clear weather forecast. Sabalenka's 2024 Rome final run underscores her growing clay prowess.

events.title: NBA Playoffs: Eastern Conference Champion

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors the New York Knicks at 53.5% implied probability to claim the Eastern Conference title, driven by their commanding 3-0 series lead over the No. 7 Philadelphia 76ers in the conference semifinals, highlighted by Jalen Brunson's 33 points and nine assists alongside Mikal Bridges' 23 points in Game 3. The Detroit Pistons sit close at 35.9% after jumping to a 2-0 advantage versus the No. 4 Cleveland Cavaliers, building on Cade Cunningham's dominant first-round performance in a historic 3-1 comeback against Orlando. The Cavaliers hold slim 8.5% odds facing potential elimination without a road win, while the 76ers languish at 1.0% on the brink of a sweep amid poor recent form. Recent injury concerns like OG Anunoby's questionable hamstring add minor uncertainty for New York ahead of Game 4.

markets.question: Liverpool FC vs. Chelsea FC: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game between Liverpool FC and Chelsea FC, scheduled for May 9 at 7:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Liverpool FC and Chelsea FC combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Liverpool host Chelsea at Anfield on May 9 in a pivotal Premier League fixture rescheduled after Chelsea's Champions League exit to PSG, confirming the 12:30pm BST kickoff amid top-four race implications. The Reds sit third with 55 points from 33 games (16-7-10), bolstered by home form in this fierce rivalry, while Chelsea languish ninth on 48 points from 34 matches (13-9-12), hampered by a four-game goalless streak and extensive injury woes including Reece James out, Levi Colwill doubtful, and potential absences for Cole Palmer and Joao Pedro. markets.question: Will Joe Flacco start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Patrick Mahomes' ongoing rehab from a torn ACL and LCL suffered in Week 15 of 2025 remains the pivotal uncertainty, with recent OTA updates from Andy Reid and GM Brett Veach confirming good progress toward full mobility and contact drills needed for Week 1 clearance, yet traders price only a slim 56.5% implied probability amid nine-month recovery timelines. The Chiefs' crowded QB room—bolstered by March trade for Justin Fields, whom Reid called a "legitimate starting NFL quarterback" ready for early-season duty, holdover practice-squad riser Chris Oladokun, veteran Joe Flacco as free-agent option, and Gardner Minshew's prior backup audition—fuels the bunched top outcomes around 50%, reflecting skin-in-the-game consensus on potential training camp hiccups or cautious medical clearance delaying Mahomes' return.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Buenos Aires be 9°C on May 9?
markets.description:

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Minister Pistarini Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ar/ezeiza/SAEZ.

Gelabert International Airport station. Recent Instituto de Meteorología e Hidrología de Panamá (IMHEPA) updates highlight fewer scattered showers (aguaceros dispersos) than earlier in the week, enabling stronger solar insolation and convective inhibition in the tropical climate, where May averages hover around 31°C. This positioning reflects robust agreement across forecast ensembles, with minimal cloud cover expected. Realistic challenges include unexpected afternoon thunderstorm development, which could introduce shading and precipitation to cap temperatures below 33°C before daily maximum observation.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $5,700 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.
